title: Help with calendar view
excerpt:
I want to have a calendar that displays deadlines (several from each project). 
I’m trying to think of a way to do this where the calendar will show me what each deadline is referring to (see pictures). Any ideas?

posts.author: Bree (Bree)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to have a calendar that displays deadlines (several from each project).

I’m trying to think of a way to do this where the calendar will show me what each deadline is referring to (see pictures). Any ideas?

mirror columns1171×441 45.5 KB

image1144×654 91.5 KB

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bree,
You could create a new date column exclusively for your major deadlines and then set up a Calendar Widget to display these dates only.
For instance, if you add a “Major Deadline” column, you can populate it with the important dates from each project. As such by selecting only this column in your Calendar Widget, you ensure that less critical dates are filtered out while showing all significant deadlines.
FYI you could also color-code these deadlines based on project type to make it easier to identify them at a glance
Cheers,
Harsh
Fario Consulting

posts.author: Bree (Bree)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much for your reply!
Let’s assume each project will have three major deadlines and each project will be the same type. I want to use the calendar to view ALL of these deadlines on the calendar. I need to know at a glance what each deadline is for.
I’m hoping to make this work in Monday so that I don’t have to manually update an external calendar every time a Monday board deadline changes.

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Bree,

Is Colour by → Timeline column what you’re looking for? It gives events colours based on the column the date was sourced from. The option is a bit hidden. I hope that’s it!

If that’s not enough information, you can try Calendar Plus. We’re soon adding our “information at a glance” feature from our week view to our month view as well. This feature shows other bits of information from your items on events in the calendar. (To colour by column, just select no column to colour by.)

Reinier

posts.author: Bree (Bree)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah! I found Colour by → Timeline column ! It was hiding at the bottom. Thank you so much!!!

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, @Bree @Fario_Consulting! We’ve developed a new calendar app packed with these features and much more. check it out: monday.com apps marketplace
